place: Brigantine city, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Brigantine’s boardwalk, built in 1906, stretches close to the Atlantic Ocean, making it more vulnerable to storm damage than its neighbor’s boardwalk.
The city’s boardwalk suffered heavy losses during the 1944 Great Atlantic hurricane, with only a few sections surviving until the 1962 Ash Wednesday Storm.
Brigantine’s name honors Margate in Kent, England, reflecting the town’s early settlers’ heritage.
The city sits on Absecon Island, an 8.1‑mile stretch that also hosts Atlantic City and Ventnor City to the northeast.
Local marinas offer docks for lease, equipment rentals, fuel stations, and fishing charters, catering to boating enthusiasts.
Lucy the Elephant, a 65‑foot wooden and metal‑clad elephant, stands as America’s oldest surviving roadside attraction and a National Historic Landmark.
